html:
Exclusive to:

Sea Bones are naturally spawning objects exclusive to Don't Starve Together, introduced in Return of Them, and found exclusively on the Lunar Island in the Rocky Beach Biome.

They can be hammered to obtain 2 Bone Shards plus 50% to get an extra one.

Trivia

  • Sea Bones were introduced in the Turn of Tides update.
DSTDSRoGSWHam

“That's what they get for coming up on land.”
–Wilson
“GROSS! What if I'd stepped on it by accident?”
–Willow
“Big fish is very dead.”
–Wolfgang
“The sea rejects its dead.”
–Wendy
“THAT'S WHAT YOU GET FOR LIVING IN THE OCEAN”
–WX-78
“What curious skeletal structures.”
–Wickerbottom
“Smells ripe.”
–Woodie
“Adapt or perish.”
–Maxwell
“Njörd's depths höld many secrets.”
–Wigfrid
“Yuck! Dead stuff.”
–Webber
“Hmm... that might be good in a soup.”
–Warly
“Dead?”
–Wormwood
“They're picked clean dry.”
–Winona
“Bones from the sea, these look to be!”
–Wortox
“Poor fish, shoulda stayed in water, florp.”
–Wurt
“These are definitely sea monster bones!”
–Walter
“Not what I was hoping to see on my stroll down the beach.”
–Wanda
Sea Bones are naturally spawning objects exclusive to Don't Starve Together, introduced in Return of Them, and found exclusively on the Lunar Island in the Rocky Beach Biome.
They can be hammered to obtain 2 Bone Shards plus 50% to get an extra one.
Trivia[edit | edit source]
Sea Bones were introduced in the Turn of Tides update.

'''Sea Bones''' are naturally spawning objects exclusive to ''[[Don't Starve Together]]'', introduced in ''[[Return of Them]]'', and found exclusively on the [[Lunar Island]] in the [[Rocky Beach]] [[Biome]].

They can be hammered to obtain 2 [[Bone Shards]] plus 50% to get an extra one.

== {{Pic|32|Placeholder}} Trivia ==
* Sea Bones were introduced in the ''[[Return of Them#Turn of Tides|Turn of Tides]]'' update.
